fields.summary: Enhancement request: Slack notifications when quality gate fails on main branch
fields.description: We would like SonarQube to automatically send a Slack notification to our team channel when the quality gate fails on a main branch analysis. Currently developers have to manually check the SonarQube dashboard or wait for the Jenkins pipeline email to know about quality gate failures. By the time someone checks, the issue has sometimes been sitting there for hours. We want a real-time alert in our existing Slack channel hash engineering-alerts whenever the quality gate status changes from passed to failed on main or release branches. Ideally the notification should include the project name, the failing condition, and a direct link to the SonarQube dashboard.

content.text: We are aware that SonarQube has a webhooks feature. We are not sure if this can be used to trigger Slack messages directly or if we need middleware. We also want to know if there is a SonarQube Slack plugin available for our version 10.2.

Arjun, good suggestion. There are two approaches. Option 1: Use SonarQube webhooks to post to a small middleware service that formats the payload and forwards to Slack via Incoming Webhook. This gives full control over the message format. Option 2: The SonarQube Slack plugin from the marketplace supports SonarQube 9+ and 10+ and can post directly to a Slack channel when quality gate status changes. I will check if we can install the plugin on our instance — plugin installations require a server restart so I need to schedule a maintenance window.
Option 2 with the plugin sounds ideal. We prefer not to maintain a middleware service. Is there an ETA on when the plugin could be installed? We would be happy to test it in the next sprint.

Scheduled the plugin installation for the maintenance window on Saturday 23rd November. Will update this ticket after installation. If the plugin works well for your team we can announce it as available to all teams. Keeping ticket open until installation and validation is complete.
